title: Apparatus, assembly and process for disinfection of foodstuffs
abstract: An apparatus an a method for disinfecting products is disclosed herein. The apparatus comprises a tank for holding a liquid for bathing the products therein, the tank comprising a channel for the liquid to enable a product to be carried into, along, and out of the channel. The apparatus also comprises at least one ultrasonic transducer arranged to be at least partially submerged in the liquid for providing ultrasonic energy via the liquid to a product bathed in the liquid in the channel for forcing microorganisms off the product and into the liquid, and a heater arranged to heat the liquid in the channel to a temperature for killing microorganisms in the liquid.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1 . An apparatus for disinfecting products, the apparatus comprising: a tank for holding a liquid for bathing the products therein, the tank comprising a channel for the liquid to enable a product to be carried into, along, and out of the channel; at least one ultrasonic transducer arranged to be at least partially submerged in the liquid for providing ultrasonic energy via the liquid to a product bathed in the liquid in the channel for forcing microorganisms off the product and into the liquid; and a heater arranged to heat the liquid in the channel to a temperature for killing microorganisms in the liquid. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 wherein the heater is at least partially submerged in the liquid at a shallower depth than that of the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 3. The apparatus of any of the previous claims wherein the heater is at least partially separated from the liquid in the channel by a guard, wherein the guard is arranged to convey any bubbles produced by the heater in the liquid away from the channel. 4. The apparatus of claim 3 wherein the guard is arranged to convey any bubbles produced by the heater in the liquid away from the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 5. The apparatus of claim 3 or 4 wherein the guard extends to the surface of the liquid thereby forming two regions in the tank, a first region in which the heater is submerged, and a second region providing the channel for the product to pass therethrough. 6. The apparatus of claim 1 wherein the heater is outside the tank and arranged to supply a source of hot liquid to the channel in the tank. 7. The apparatus of any of the previous claims wherein the heater is arranged to heat the liquid in the tank to at least 60 Â°C. 8. The apparatus of any of the previous claims comprising a flow provider arranged to recirculate liquid from a region at the top of the tank to a region at the bottom of the tank. 5 9. The apparatus of any of the previous claims comprising at least a pair of opposing ultrasonic transducers arranged either side of the channel. 10. The apparatus of any of the previous claims comprising an ultrasonic transducer arranged to provide ultrasonic energy upwards towards products in the channel from 10 beneath the channel. 1 1 . The apparatus of any of the previous claims comprising a conveyor for carrying the product through the liquid. 15 12. The apparatus of claim 1 1 wherein the conveyor is configured to bathe the product in the liquid for at least 2.5 seconds. 13. The apparatus of claim 1 1 or 12 wherein the conveyor is configured to bathe the product in the liquid for less than 30 seconds. 20 14. The apparatus of claim 1 1 , 12 or 13 wherein the conveyor is arranged to carry the product through the liquid at a location in line with the centre of the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 25 15. The apparatus of any of the previous claims wherein the ultrasonic transducer is coupled to a wall of the tank inside the tank, thereby forming a boundary of the liquid in the tank. 16. The apparatus of any of the previous claims comprising a plurality of ultrasonic 30 transducers arranged along the length of channel. 17. The apparatus of claim 16 wherein the plurality of ultrasonic transducers are phase linked and/or synchronised, for example synchronised in frequency. 18. The apparatus of any of the previous claims further comprising an ultrasonic power oscillator coupled to the at least one ultrasonic transducer for driving the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 5 19. The apparatus of any of the previous claims comprising a liquid level sensor configured to provide an indication of the level of the liquid in the tank. 20. The apparatus of claim 19 comprising a feeder arranged to top-up the level of the 10 liquid in the tank in response to the liquid level sensor indicating that the level of the liquid in the tank falls below a selected threshold. 21 . The apparatus of claim 19 or 20 arranged to turn off at least one of the heater and the at least one ultrasonic transducer in response to the liquid level sensor indicating that 15 the level of the liquid in the tank is above a selected threshold. 22. The apparatus of claim 19, 20 or 21 arranged to switch on at least one of the heater and the at least one ultrasonic transducer in response to the liquid level sensor indicating that the level of the liquid in the tank falls below a selected threshold. 20 23. The apparatus of any of the previous claims comprising a skimmer arranged to syphon off some of the liquid in the tank from a region near the top of the channel at selected intervals. 25 24. The apparatus of claim 23 wherein the skimmer comprises a filter for removing oils, fats and/or other debris from the liquid. 25. The apparatus of claim 23 or 24 wherein the apparatus is arranged to replenish the liquid in the tank as liquid is removed by the skimmer, optionally wherein the 30 replenished liquid is heated prior to circulation into the tank. 26. The apparatus of any of the previous claims further comprising an outlet at the bottom of the tank for draining the liquid from the tank, wherein the outlet is arranged to 

 filter and heat the liquid drained from the tank and recirculate the filtered and heated liquid to the tank. 27. The apparatus of any of the previous claims wherein the at least one ultrasonic 5 transducer comprises a heat sink for removing heat from the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 28. The apparatus of claim 27 wherein the heat sink comprises a radiator for radiating heat away from the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 10 29. The apparatus of claim 27 or 28 wherein the tank comprises a cut-out region exposing at least a portion of the ultrasonic transducer outside the tank. 30. The apparatus of claim 29 wherein the portion of the ultrasonic transducer outside 15 the tank is a face of an assembly enclosing the ultrasonic transducer. 31 . The apparatus of any of the previous claims wherein the liquid is water. 32. The apparatus of any of the previous claims further comprising a programmable 20 logic circuit, PLC, configured to control at least one of: the delivery of ultrasonic energy to the liquid, the speed of the conveyor and the temperature of the liquid. 33. The apparatus of any of the previous claims wherein the apparatus is configured to disinfect foodstuffs, for example meat such as chicken or beef, fruit and vegetables 25 and nuts. 34. The apparatus of any of the previous claims wherein the tank is insulated to retain heat in the liquid in the tank. 30 35. An apparatus substantially as described herein with reference to the accompanying drawings. 36. An assembly for disinfecting products, comprising a plurality of the apparatus of 

 any of the previous claims each arranged to provide a module of the assembly, wherein each module is configured to be coupled together by a water-tight seal and arranged to allow the passage of liquid and products from one module of the assembly to another module of the assembly. 37. An assembly substantially as described herein with reference to the accompanying drawings. 38. A method for disinfecting products, the method comprising: holding a liquid in a tank for bathing the products therein, the tank comprising a channel for the liquid to enable a product to be carried into, along, and out of the channel; delivering ultrasonic energy via the liquid to a product bathed in the liquid in the channel to force microorganisms off the product and into the liquid; and heating the liquid in the channel to a temperature for killing microorganisms in the liquid. 39. The method of claim 38 comprising heating the liquid in the channel via a heater that is at least partially submerged in the liquid at a shallower depth than that of the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 40. The method of claim 39 wherein the heater is at least partially separated from the liquid in the channel by a guard, wherein the guard is arranged to convey any bubbles produced by the heater in the liquid away from the channel. 41 . The method of claim 40 wherein the guard is arranged to convey any bubbles produced by the heater in the liquid away from the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 42. The method of claim 40 or 41 wherein the guard extends to the surface of the liquid thereby forming two regions in the tank, a first region in which the heater is submerged, and a second region providing the channel for the product to pass therethrough. 43. The method of claim 38 comprising heating the liquid in the tank via a heater outside the tank and supplying the heated liquid to the channel in the tank. 44. The method of any of claims 38 to 43 comprising heating the liquid in the tank to 5 at least 60 Â°C. 45. The method of any of claims 38 to 44 comprising recirculating liquid from a region at the top of the tank to a region at the bottom of the tank. 10 46. The method of any of claims 38 to 45 comprising delivering ultrasonic energy to the product from at least a pair of opposing ultrasonic transducers arranged either side of the channel. 47. The method of any of claims 38 to 46 comprising delivering ultrasonic energy to 15 the product upwards towards products in the channel from beneath the channel. 48. The method of any of claims 38 to 47 comprising carrying the product through the liquid. 20 49. The method of claim 48 wherein carrying the product through the liquid comprises bathing the product in the liquid for at least 2.5 seconds. 50. The method of claim 48 or 49 wherein carrying the product through the liquid comprises bathing the product in the liquid for less than 30 seconds. 25 51 . The method of claim 48, 49 or 50 comprising carrying the product through the liquid at a location in line with the centre of the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 52. The method of any of claims 38 to 51 wherein the ultrasonic transducer is 30 coupled to a wall of the tank inside the tank, thereby forming a boundary of the liquid in the tank. 53. The method of any of claims 38 to 52 comprising delivery ultrasonic energy to the 

 product from a plurality of ultrasonic transducers arranged along the length of channel. 54. The method of claim 53 wherein the plurality of ultrasonic transducers are phase linked and/or synchronised, for example synchronised in frequency. 55. The method of any of claims 38 to 54 comprising sensing the level of the liquid in the tank. 56. The method of claim 55 comprising topping-up the level of the liquid in the tank in response to sensing that the level of the liquid in the tank falls below a selected threshold. 57. The method of claim 55 or 56 further comprising turning off at least one of the heater and the at least one ultrasonic transducer in response to sensing that the level of the liquid in the tank falls below a selected threshold. 58. The method of claim 55, 56 or 57 arranged to switch on at least one of the heater and the at least one ultrasonic transducer in response to the liquid level sensor indicating that the level of the liquid in the tank is above a selected threshold. 59. The method of any of claims 38 to 58 comprising syphoning off some of the liquid in the tank from a region near the top of the channel at selected intervals. 60. The method of claim 59 comprising removing oils, fats and/or other debris from the liquid. 61 . The method of claim 59 or 60 comprising replenishing the liquid in the tank as liquid is syphoned off, optionally wherein the replenished liquid is heated prior to circulation in the tank. 62. The method of any of claims 38 to 61 comprising draining the liquid from an outlet at the bottom of the tank, filtering and heating the liquid, and recirculating the filtered and heated liquid to the tank. 63. The method of any of claims 38 to 62 comprising cooling the at least one ultrasonic transducer via a heat sink. 5 64. The method of claim 63 wherein the heat sink comprises a radiator for radiating heat away from the at least one ultrasonic transducer. 65. The method of claim 63 or 64 wherein cooling the at least one ultrasonic transducer comprises exposing at least a portion of the ultrasonic transducer outside the 10 tank. 66. The method of claim 65 wherein the portion of the ultrasonic transducer outside the tank is a face of an assembly enclosing the ultrasonic transducer. 15 67. The method of any of claims 38 to 66 wherein the liquid is water. 68. The method of any of claims 38 to 67 comprising controlling at least one of: the delivery of ultrasonic energy to the liquid, the speed of the conveyor and the temperature of the liquid via a programmable logic circuit, PLC. 20 69. The method of any of claims 38 to 68 wherein the method is a method for disinfecting foodstuffs, for example meat such as chicken or beef, fruit and vegetables and nuts. 25 70. The method of any of claims 38 to 69 wherein the tank is insulated to retain heat in the liquid in the tank. 71 . A method substantially as described herein with reference to the accompanying drawings.
